medical_history.chief_complaint: small hyperkeratotic lesions on her left lower extremity from the age of 13 years
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Hyperkeratotic papules a few millimeters in diameter that were polygonal and sharply demarcated. They were brown and slightly depressed in the center. The lesions were situated on the left lower extremity forming a strip approximately 15 cm wide that began in the external iliac crest and continued on the anterior and inner thigh down the inner leg to the left ankle.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Biopsy specimens showed a hyperkeratotic laminar lesion that stained strongly eosinophilic in contrast with the basket-weave stratum corneum surrounding the lesion. The epidermis under the lesion was atrophic, and the granular layer was diminished. Slight acanthosis was present at the borders of the lesion, but no alterations were seen in the dermis.

other_tests.findings: Hyperkeratotic laminar lesion that stained strongly eosinophilic in contrast with the basket-weave stratum corneum surrounding the lesion. The epidermis under the lesion was atrophic, and the granular layer was diminished. Slight acanthosis was present at the borders of the lesion, but no alterations were seen in the dermis.
Laminar hyperkeratotic lesions with persistence of desmosomes and degenerated cytoplasmic structures in the stratum corneum. There was also atrophy of the epidermis and occasional Odland bodies.

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was made based on a combination of clinical, histopathologic, and ultrastructural findings. Clinical examination showed hyperkeratotic papules on the left lower extremity. Histopathologic study revealed compact orthokeratotic hyperkeratosis, often with areas of parakeratosis, and the stratum granulosum was absent or reduced. Ultrastructural study showed laminar hyperkeratotic lesions with persistence of desmosomes and degenerated cytoplasmic structures in the stratum corneum, along with atrophy of the epidermis and occasional Odland bodies.
